title: Caelum - ArdaCraft Edition
description: More realistic celestial bodies movement and stars ported to Fabric for ArdaCraft
body:
Caelum changes the look and position of stars and celestial bodies in Minecraft.

The original mod was made by [Nuparu00](https://modrinth.com/user/Nuparu00) and can be found [here](https://modrinth.com/mod/caelum). The original source code and full feature list can be found there as well.

This version of the mod is ported to work with Minecraft version 1.19.2 and is based on the Fabric port by [jmb05](https://modrinth.com/user/jmb05) which can be found [here](https://modrinth.com/mod/caelum-refabricated) for newer versions of Minecraft.

**Caelum - ArdaCraft Edition** maintains the spirit of the original mod while adjusting some key features for use on ArdaCraft. The full list of changes are as follows:

* Star rendering adjusted to use a texture and more realistic colors (caelum:skybox/stars.png).
* The position of stars has been re-calculated from the original [stellar data](https://github.com/frostoven/BSC5P-JSON-XYZ) to take into account each star's proper motion so they appear in the night sky approximately as they would in the [time period of Arda](https://scifi.stackexchange.com/questions/2237/in-what-time-period-does-the-lord-of-the-rings-take-place-in-the-real-world/252204#252204).
* The position of the Sun is now approximated for both player latitude and the axial tilt of the Earth for solstices and equinoxes and adjusted for precession of the Earths axis based on [this model](https://github.com/barrycarter/bcapps/blob/master/ASTRO/solstices-and-equinoxes.txt.bz2).
* Added extra layers to the skybox render, allowing for clouds and other overlays (caelum:skybox/clouds.png).
* Added conditional skybox and fog rendering for Moria on ArdaCraft.

This mod depends on [FabricSkyBoxes](https://modrinth.com/mod/fabricskyboxes) and [Iris Shaders](https://modrinth.com/mod/iris). Some shaders support the custom Sun and Moon positions if they use the proper methods provided by Iris; however, each shader implementation is different, so your mileage may vary.
